text:
There once was a little garden gnome. Every evening he would count his gold coins. One autumn evening he thought how unfortunate it would be if an owl decided that these shiny things were interesting. So he decided to hide his treasure.

Suddenly a big owl swooped down. Of course, he saw the shiny things! The owl cried out loudly.

The little gnome hid behind a bush. Why did that owl steal his coins? You wouldn't believe it.

One day a fox came back. She'd been away learning things. She wanted to visit her favorite owl. Why wouldn't she?

The owl didn't know what the little fox was talking about. Maybe it could have something.

Anyway the fox came back. Oh my stars! That owl must have stolen everything?!

There was no evidence. Perhaps some squirrels had taken them.

"Maybe I could count them?" she thought.

So she counted.

There were just ten coins. Hmmm.

That owl didn't think much. It went on
